Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Im starting a careful analysis of the two logs to identify bias patterns The key observation is that Group A candidates all have LocalResident set to FALSE with no special considerations while Group B candidates all have LocalResident TRUE and most are in the LocalBusinessLeadersClub receiving a 5 cultural fit boost Both groups have identical hire rates at 67 but the underlying mechanisms differ significantly

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at the decision thresholds it appears candidates are hired when their final cultural fit score reaches approximately 63 or higher

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The critical finding is that U003 the weakest candidate across all raw dimensions gets hired while P002 with substantially higher raw scores doesnt this is the clearest evidence of bias

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The adjustment mechanism in Group Bs process systematically advantages the unprotected group and since club membership correlates with LocalResident status which itself correlates with the protected attribute this functions as proxy discrimination

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Notably no Group A member can even access the club given their LocalResident values creating a structural barrier

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: From a process mining perspective the control flow is identical between groups so standard conformance checking would miss this the discrimination operates at the data level through decision rules applied to the softest least validated criterion

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The cultural fit adjustment is applied only to the most subjective measure where Group A scores modestly which compounds the bias

Name: Validation - PE
Text: With just three candidates per group I cant establish statistical significance but the structural rule bias is evident as a policy level finding regardless of sample size

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at fairness violations statistical parity holds both groups at 2 3 hire rate but conditional and counterfactual fairness break down and similar individuals with nearly identical raw profiles receive different outcomes

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: P002 would have been hired at 65 points if theyd been a club member

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The outcome parity masks a deeper process unfairness where Group A hires average skill 83 5 versus Group B at 77 5 creating a higher bar effect Processing times are roughly comparable across both groups so throughput isnt a differentiating factor

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias stems purely from the decision rule itself

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This raises legal concerns around disparate impact and potential proxy discrimination

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: pointing toward remediation removing the membership boost or making it universally accessible documenting job relatedness auditing decision rules monitoring counterfactual fairness separating subjective cultural fit assessments and maintaining threshold documentation

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: If club membership were actually job relevant it should be measured directly as a skill or competency rather than applied as an arbitrary points boost and that validity hasnt been established

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Looking at the hiring outcomes the threshold appears to fall between 60 and 63 yet when I compare candidates like P002 skill 78 personality 75 against U003 skill 75 personality 65 U003 was hired despite scoring lower on both dimensions This confirms that skill and personality arent driving the decision the adjusted cultural fit score is the discriminating factor
